mnemonics: Gradient For Vector-Valued Functions? Just Stack Jacobians!
The Gradient's Tower: Each Floor is a Scalar Function's Gradient
keyPoints: The gradient of a vector-valued function F: R^n -> R^m is an m x n matrix (the Jacobian).
Each row of this gradient matrix is the transpose of the gradient (∇f_i) of its corresponding scalar component function.
It generalizes the concept of the derivative from scalar functions to higher dimensions.
commonMistakes: Assuming the gradient is a vector, when for a vector-valued function it is actually a matrix (the Jacobian).
Forgetting to transpose the gradients of the component functions when forming the rows of the larger gradient matrix.
